frameworks.name: computational neuroscience modeling
frameworks.one_line: Modeling practice viewed as a heterogeneous set of goal-driven activities.

frameworks.name: goal-pluralism in model evaluation
frameworks.one_line: Models judged relative to their authors' stated goals rather than a single criterion.

core_question: Do computational neuroscientists pursue a common goal, or do their modeling goals genuinely diverge in ways that affect how research should be evaluated?
key_assumptions: Modeling efforts are motivated by goals that are not always made explicit
Multiple distinct goals can each be legitimately of highest importance
Evaluation criteria for a model depend on the goal it serves
manipulations: Informal online survey of modelers' stated goals
key_findings: Survey responses confirm wide divergence of goals across the community
Disagreements about model merit often track goal differences rather than methodological flaws
interpretations: Authors should explicitly state goals when proposing models so evaluation matches intent
Apparent disputes over model quality dissolve once goal heterogeneity is acknowledged
